name: Dark circles
dek: Pigmentary, vascular, and structural dark circles look the same in the mirror but respond to entirely different things. The serum aisle treats them as one problem; we treat them as three.
oneliner: Vascular, pigmentary, structural. Treat the right one.
bottom: The most-misdiagnosed category in cosmetic dermatology. Identify which type you have first; spend the money second.

triggers.k: Skin lifts the same colour with stretch (pigmentary)
triggers.n: Tyrosinase-driven. The category most-likely to respond to a topical regimen.
Visible vessels through thin periorbital skin. Topicals will not move this — laser or filler will.
Anatomy, not pigment. The shadow is light, not melanin. Volume restoration is the only real fix.
Modulator, not cause. Sleep affects perfusion and lid puffiness, both of which deepen the appearance.

ingredients.name: Hydroquinone 2% periorbital
ingredients.evidence: Effective when pigmentation is the primary mechanism. Use only at lower strength near eyes.

ingredients.name: Vitamin K + retinol eye cream
ingredients.evidence: Modest endpoints on perfusion and lid laxity. Slow.

ingredients.name: Caffeine 5%
ingredients.evidence: Hours-long perfusion shift. Useful cosmetically; not a treatment.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic 3% periorbital
ingredients.evidence: Promising small-trial data; pairs with hydroquinone in skin of colour.

ingredients.name: Hyaluronic filler (clinic)
ingredients.evidence: Tear-trough volumisation. The single most effective intervention for true hollows.

ingredients.name: Q-switched laser (clinic)
ingredients.evidence: Effective for dermal melanin; not for vascular or structural.

phases.b: Stretch test in the mirror. Pigmentary stays brown; vascular fades; structural shifts as the light angle changes.
If pigmentary: hydroquinone 2% PM + tranexamic 3% AM, with rigorous SPF. 8 weeks is the decision point.
Vascular: KTP / pulsed-dye laser. Structural: tear-trough filler. Topicals will not move these categories.
